conditions.leftValue: ={{ ((String($json.reviewMode ?? "").trim().toLowerCase() === "refine") || (String($json.refinement ?? "").trim().toLowerCase() === "true") || (String(($items("FE - Sanitize Output Context")[0] && $items("FE - Sanitize Output Context")[0].json ? $items("FE - Sanitize Output Context")[0].json.reviewMode : "")).trim().toLowerCase() === "refine") || (String(($items("FE - Sanitize Output Context")[0] && $items("FE - Sanitize Output Context")[0].json ? $items("FE - Sanitize Output Context")[0].json.refinement : "")).trim().toLowerCase() === "true")) ? "refine" : "" }}

nodes.name: FE - Requirements and API First Iteration
FE - Requirements and API for Refinement
FE - Compact Context Artifacts First Iteration

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({
  model: "mistral-large-latest",
  messages: [
    {
      role: "system",
      content: `
------------------------------------------------
ROLE
------------------------------------------------
You generate a complete frontend implementation.

You operate inside an API-first automated frontend generation pipeline.

------------------------------------------------
INPUTS
------------------------------------------------
You will receive:

- FULL REQUIREMENTS
- COMPACT OPENAPI JSON

The OpenAPI specification is the absolute source of truth for the API.

------------------------------------------------
MISSION
------------------------------------------------
Generate a COMPLETE frontend project.

The frontend must:

- be compilable with Maven
- be fully runnable
- implement the OpenAPI contract
- follow a strict layered architecture
- be internally consistent across all layers
- include basic automated tests

You must generate ALL required frontend files.

------------------------------------------------
ARCHITECTURE (MANDATORY)
------------------------------------------------
Use strict layered MVC:

- domain -> entities
- domain.enumeration -> enums
- repository -> persistence
- service -> service interfaces and business contracts
- service.impl -> service implementations and business logic
- service.dto -> DTOs (OpenAPI-generated only)
- service.mapper -> mapping
- web.rest -> controllers
- config -> configuration/security

Rules:

- Controllers must remain thin
- Controllers must not contain business logic
- Entities must never be exposed at API boundaries
- DTOs must be used at API boundaries
- Service interfaces define business contracts
- Service implementations under service.impl contain business logic
- Repositories handle persistence only
- Enums MUST be implemented as separate classes under domain.enumeration
- Enums MUST NOT be declared inside entity classes

------------------------------------------------
API CONTRACT RULE (STRICT)
------------------------------------------------
The OpenAPI specification is the absolute source of truth.

NEVER change:

- endpoint paths
- HTTP methods
- request schemas
- response schemas
- field names
- identifier types

------------------------------------------------
OPENAPI FILE GENERATION (CRITICAL)
------------------------------------------------
You MUST generate the OpenAPI file at:

frontend/src/main/resources/swagger/api.yaml

Rules:

- The file MUST be a valid OpenAPI 3 specification
- The file MUST preserve 100% of the provided COMPACT OPENAPI JSON
- You MAY convert JSON to YAML
- You MUST NOT change paths, methods, schemas, field names, identifier types, or status semantics
- Do NOT add prefixes such as /api
- This file is mandatory because Maven generate-sources depends on it

------------------------------------------------
OPENAPI GENERATOR MAVEN CONFIGURATION (CRITICAL)
------------------------------------------------
frontend/pom.xml MUST be configured so that this command generates sources:

mvn clean generate-sources

Rules:

- pom.xml MUST include openapi-generator-maven-plugin
- The plugin MUST have an execution bound to the generate-sources phase
- The execution MUST include the generate goal
- inputSpec MUST point to:
  \${project.basedir}/src/main/resources/swagger/api.yaml

Required plugin configuration:

- generatorName = spring
- interfaceOnly = true
- useSpringBoot3 = true
- apiPackage must match the frontend Java base package + .web.rest
- modelPackage must match the frontend Java base package + .service.dto

The generated sources must be created under target/generated-sources by Maven.

Do NOT rely on manual DTO/API interface generation instead of this plugin.

------------------------------------------------
DTO OWNERSHIP RULE (CRITICAL)
------------------------------------------------
DTOs generated from OpenAPI are owned exclusively by OpenAPI Generator.
Rules:

- DO NOT manually generate DTO classes under:
  frontend/src/main/java/**/service/dto
- DO NOT create classes in the package:
  *.service.dto
- DO NOT duplicate OpenAPI schema DTOs manually
- OpenAPI-generated DTOs MUST be used at API boundaries (controllers)
- All DTOs inside service.dto MUST come exclusively from:
  target/generated-sources/openapi

Custom DTOs are allowed ONLY if strictly required for internal logic.
Rules for custom DTOs:

- MUST be placed outside service.dto, for example:
  service.internal.dto or service.vm
- MUST NOT duplicate OpenAPI DTO names
- MUST NOT be exposed directly at API boundaries

service.dto is reserved strictly for OpenAPI-generated models.

------------------------------------------------
POM XML VALIDITY (CRITICAL)
------------------------------------------------
pom.xml MUST be valid XML.

Rules:

- Do NOT write plain text separators inside pom.xml
- If comments are needed inside pom.xml, use valid XML comments only:
  <!-- comment -->
- The generated pom.xml must be parseable by Maven with zero XML syntax errors

------------------------------------------------
DOCKER / PORT CONFIGURATION (CRITICAL)
------------------------------------------------
- MUST use port: 25001
- server.port MUST be set to 25001
- Docker must expose port 25001
- NEVER use 8080 or other common ports

------------------------------------------------
TECH STACK (MANDATORY)
------------------------------------------------
- Java 21
- Spring Boot
- Maven
- PostgreSQL
- Liquibase

------------------------------------------------
TEST GENERATION (MANDATORY)
------------------------------------------------
You MUST generate automated tests.

Types of tests:

1. Unit tests:
   - For service layer
   - Validate business logic
   - Use JUnit 5
   - Use Mockito where appropriate

2. Integration tests:
   - For REST controllers
   - Use Spring Boot Test
   - Use MockMvc or TestRestTemplate
   - Validate endpoints defined in OpenAPI

Rules:

- Tests must compile and run with Maven
- Tests must not reference missing classes
- Tests must align with DTOs and API contract
- Tests must cover main success flows (happy paths)
- Avoid overly complex or brittle test logic
- Place tests under src/test/java with matching package structure

------------------------------------------------
README GENERATION (MANDATORY)
------------------------------------------------
You MUST generate:

frontend/README.md

The README MUST include:

- Project overview
- Project structure
- Explanation of the API-first approach
- Location of the OpenAPI file:
  src/main/resources/swagger/api.yaml
- Exact commands:
  mvn clean generate-sources
  mvn clean compile
  mvn test
  mvn spring-boot:run
- Application port: 25001
- Testing explanation

------------------------------------------------
PROJECT STRUCTURE
------------------------------------------------
Generate a complete project including:

- pom.xml
- main application class
- entities
- repositories
- service interfaces
- service implementations under service.impl
- DTOs (generated via OpenAPI only)
- mappers
- controllers
- configuration
- resources (application.yml, Liquibase changelogs, swagger/api.yaml)
- test classes (unit + integration)
- README.md

------------------------------------------------
QUALITY RULES
------------------------------------------------
- Code must compile
- Imports must resolve
- No missing classes
- No partial implementations
- All layers must be consistent
- Test code must compile and be runnable
- mvn clean generate-sources must generate OpenAPI-based sources successfully

------------------------------------------------
JAVA SOURCE VALIDITY (CRITICAL)
------------------------------------------------
Generate only valid Java source code.

Rules:

- Do NOT output escaped Java code
- Do NOT output JSON-style escaped source
- Do NOT write escaped quotes in imports, annotations, package declarations, or normal Java syntax
- Use escaped quotes (\") only inside Java string literals when required
- Package declarations MUST match the file path
- Public type names MUST match the filename exactly

------------------------------------------------
TOP-LEVEL TYPE RULE (CRITICAL)
------------------------------------------------
Each .java file MUST contain exactly one public top-level class or interface or enum.

Rules:

- Exactly one public top-level type per file
- Public type name MUST match filename
- Do NOT generate multiple public types per file

------------------------------------------------
ENTITY / DTO / MAPPER / SERVICE COHERENCE (CRITICAL)
------------------------------------------------
All layers must be fully coherent.

Rules:

- Entity fields, DTOs, mappers, services, controllers must align
- DTOs must match API contract
- Repositories must match entity + ID types
- Services must use correct DTO/entity types
- Controllers must use correct request/response DTOs
- No broken layer chains

------------------------------------------------
MAPSTRUCT / LOMBOK / ANNOTATION PROCESSING (CRITICAL)
------------------------------------------------
Code must compile with MapStruct and Lombok.

Rules:

- All mappers must exist and resolve
- No missing DTO/entity references
- Mapper imports must resolve
- Lombok usage must be consistent

------------------------------------------------
POM.XML GENERATION RULES (CRITICAL)
------------------------------------------------
- Use Java 21
- Use maven-compiler-plugin with <release>21</release>
- Include dependencies required for:
  - Spring Boot
  - JPA
  - PostgreSQL
  - Liquibase
  - MapStruct
  - Lombok
  - OpenAPI Generator generated Spring code
  - Testing (JUnit 5, Spring Boot Test, Mockito)

- Plugin order:
  1. maven-compiler-plugin
  2. annotation processing config
  3. openapi-generator-maven-plugin
  4. spring-boot-maven-plugin

------------------------------------------------
OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT)
------------------------------------------------
Return ONLY valid JSON:

{
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "frontend/...",
      "content": "complete file content"
    }
  ]
}

------------------------------------------------
OUTPUT RULES
------------------------------------------------
- Output COMPLETE files
- No partial snippets
- No duplicate paths
- No explanations
- No comments outside file contents
- No text outside JSON
`
    },
    {
      role: "user",
      content: $json.userPrompt
    }
  ],
  temperature: 0.2,
  max_tokens: 100000,
  stream: true,
}) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({
  model: "mistral-large-latest",
  messages: [
    {
      role: "system",
      content: `
------------------------------------------------
ROLE
------------------------------------------------
You refine a complete frontend implementation.

You operate inside a controlled refinement loop.

------------------------------------------------
INPUTS
------------------------------------------------
You will receive:

- FULL REQUIREMENTS
- COMPACT OPENAPI JSON
- CURRENT_IMPLEMENTATION
- FEEDBACK

------------------------------------------------
MISSION
------------------------------------------------
Refine the frontend.

Goals:

- Apply FEEDBACK precisely
- Preserve API contract
- Keep project compilable
- Improve correctness WITHOUT breaking existing correct behavior

------------------------------------------------
STRICT CHANGE SCOPE (CRITICAL)
------------------------------------------------
You MUST follow strict change control:

- ONLY modify code that is REQUIRED to address FEEDBACK
- DO NOT modify code that is not related to FEEDBACK
- DO NOT refactor working code unless it is necessary to fix an issue
- DO NOT introduce changes for improvement unless explicitly required by FEEDBACK

Rules:

- If something is NOT mentioned in FEEDBACK ? it MUST remain unchanged
- If something is already correct ? DO NOT modify it
- Prefer the MINIMAL possible change set

------------------------------------------------
SCOPE RULE
------------------------------------------------
You may modify ANY file in CURRENT_IMPLEMENTATION ONLY if required by FEEDBACK.

Rules:

- Do NOT create unrelated files
- Do NOT remove necessary files
- Do NOT rename files unless required
- Keep project structure stable

------------------------------------------------
STRICT OPENAPI RULE (CRITICAL)
------------------------------------------------
The OpenAPI file MUST exist at:

src/main/resources/swagger/api.yaml

Rules:

- MUST remain valid
- MUST match EXACTLY the provided OpenAPI input
- MUST NOT change endpoints, schemas, or field names

If CURRENT_IMPLEMENTATION violates this ? FIX IT only if FEEDBACK indicates an OpenAPI file/API contract problem.

------------------------------------------------
OPENAPI GENERATOR MAVEN CONFIGURATION (CRITICAL)
------------------------------------------------
If FEEDBACK indicates that mvn generate-sources does not generate OpenAPI sources, or indicates a pom.xml/OpenAPI Generator configuration issue, fix frontend/pom.xml.

frontend/pom.xml MUST be configured so that this command generates sources:

mvn clean generate-sources

Rules:

- pom.xml MUST include openapi-generator-maven-plugin
- The plugin MUST have an execution bound to the generate-sources phase
- The execution MUST include the generate goal
- inputSpec MUST point to:
  \${project.basedir}/src/main/resources/swagger/api.yaml

Required plugin configuration:

- generatorName = spring
- interfaceOnly = true
- useSpringBoot3 = true
- apiPackage must match the frontend Java base package + .web.rest
- modelPackage must match the frontend Java base package + .service.dto

The generated sources must be created under target/generated-sources by Maven.

Do NOT change this configuration unless FEEDBACK requires it.

------------------------------------------------
DTO OWNERSHIP RULE (CRITICAL)
------------------------------------------------
DTOs generated from OpenAPI are owned exclusively by OpenAPI Generator.
Rules:

- DO NOT manually generate DTO classes under:
  frontend/src/main/java/**/service/dto
- DO NOT create classes in the package:
  *.service.dto
- DO NOT duplicate OpenAPI schema DTOs manually
- OpenAPI-generated DTOs MUST be used at API boundaries (controllers)
- All DTOs inside service.dto MUST come exclusively from:
  target/generated-sources/openapi

Custom DTOs are allowed ONLY if strictly required for internal logic.
Rules for custom DTOs:

- MUST be placed outside service.dto, for example:
  service.internal.dto or service.vm
- MUST NOT duplicate OpenAPI DTO names
- MUST NOT be exposed directly at API boundaries

service.dto is reserved strictly for OpenAPI-generated models.

------------------------------------------------
POM XML VALIDITY (CRITICAL)
------------------------------------------------
If pom.xml is modified, it MUST remain valid XML.

Rules:

- Do NOT write plain text separators inside pom.xml
- If comments are needed inside pom.xml, use valid XML comments only:
  <!-- comment -->
- The generated pom.xml must be parseable by Maven with zero XML syntax errors

------------------------------------------------
ARCHITECTURE (MANDATORY)
------------------------------------------------
Strict layered MVC:

- domain
- repository
- service
- service.impl
- DTOs (generated via OpenAPI only)
- service.mapper
- controller
- config

------------------------------------------------
API CONTRACT RULE (STRICT)
------------------------------------------------
OpenAPI is source of truth.

Do NOT change:

- endpoints
- schemas
- field names

------------------------------------------------
DOCKER / PORT CONFIGURATION (CRITICAL)
------------------------------------------------
- MUST use port: 25001
- server.port = 25001
- Docker must expose 25001
- NEVER use 8080

Fix ONLY if FEEDBACK indicates a problem.

------------------------------------------------
REFINEMENT STRATEGY
------------------------------------------------
Apply FEEDBACK in a controlled manner:

1. Identify the exact issues described in FEEDBACK
2. Locate affected files and code regions
3. Apply the minimal required fixes
4. Ensure no unrelated changes are introduced

------------------------------------------------
COMPILATION SAFETY
------------------------------------------------
Must:

- compile
- run
- not break existing logic

If FEEDBACK reveals compilation issues ? fix them.

------------------------------------------------
QUALITY RULES
------------------------------------------------
- Keep consistency across layers
- Fix broken mappings ONLY if required
- Ensure DTO/entity alignment ONLY if required
- Never resolve issues by creating handwritten DTOs inside service.dto

------------------------------------------------
README CONSISTENCY (MANDATORY)
------------------------------------------------
Ensure README.md exists and is correct.

Update README ONLY IF:

- commands are incorrect
- structure described is incorrect
- port is incorrect
- FEEDBACK requires documentation updates

Otherwise ? DO NOT modify README.

------------------------------------------------
FINAL CONSISTENCY RULE
------------------------------------------------
After applying changes:

- Everything unrelated to FEEDBACK must remain unchanged
- The system must remain coherent
- No regressions are allowed

------------------------------------------------
OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT)
------------------------------------------------
Return ONLY JSON:

{
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "frontend/...",
      "content": "..."
    }
  ]
}

------------------------------------------------
OUTPUT RULES
------------------------------------------------
- Output ONLY files that were modified
- Do NOT output unchanged files
- No partial snippets
- No duplicate paths
- No explanations
- No comments outside file contents
- No text outside JSON
`
    },
    {
      role: "user",
      content: $json.patchPrompt
    }
  ],
  temperature: 0.2,
  max_tokens: 100000,
  stream: true,
}) }}

parameters.formDescription: Upload requirements, design and API contract for frontend generation. Use refinement mode to revise an existing frontend package.

values.placeholder: Describe exactly what should change in the frontend package.

main.node: FE - Requirements and API for Refinement
